title: Advocacy * Hunger/Food Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Advocacy * Hunger/Food Issues 1 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Disabilities Issues Domestic Violence Issues Food Stamps/SNAP Recipients Former Foster Children Foster Children Hunger/Food Issues Sexual Assault Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO SNAP AND FOOD ACCESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The 211info SNAP and Food Access program provides specialized information, referral, advocacy, follow-up services, and extra guidance to individuals and family members who would like to access the SNAP program, have a question or issue with their current SNAP case, and/or need extra guidance accessing food resources. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details No Mapping Information Available
